summary.scholar: On May 20-21, 1927, Charles Lindbergh completed the first solo nonstop transatlantic flight, flying his single-engine plane Spirit of St. Louis from New York to Paris in 33.5 hours. Lindbergh became an instant international celebrity and American hero. The flight demonstrated the potential of long-distance aviation and sparked massive public interest in air travel.
summary.explorer: On May 20, 1927, a 25-year-old pilot named Charles Lindbergh flew his little plane all the way from New York to Paris — alone, without stopping! It took 33 and a half hours. He became the most famous person in the world overnight! His flight showed everyone that airplanes could go anywhere.

significance.scholar: Lindbergh's flight captured the world's imagination and accelerated the development of commercial aviation, transforming air travel from a novelty into a practical mode of transportation.
significance.explorer: Lindbergh proved that planes could cross oceans. Within a few decades, millions of people would fly across the Atlantic like it was no big deal.

sources.citation: Miller Center: U.S. Presidents and Key Events
